page: Microsoft Azure EC96as_v6 is an ECasv6-family virtual machine instance configured with 96 dedicated vCPUs. Powered by an AMD processor using the x86_64 architecture, this instance runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 672.0 GB of system memory, yielding a ratio of 7.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.

This instance does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external block storage. It is designated as Premium Storage capable, allowing connection to high-performance persistent disks. There are zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this configuration.

With its high memory-to-vCPU ratio, the EC96as_v6 provides significant memory density suitable for memory-intensive workloads. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing capabilities without resource contention. This profile is designed for workloads such as large-scale databases, in-memory caching, enterprise applications, and data analytics.
description: Microsoft Azure EC96as_v6 is an ECasv6-family virtual machine instance featuring 96 dedicated vCPUs and 672.0 GB of RAM. Built on the x86_64 architecture with AMD processors, the instance operates under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 7.0 GB per vCPU, making it highly suitable for memory-intensive operations. The instance does not include local storage but is Premium Storage capable, requiring external network-attached storage for persistent data. With zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses, network configurations must be managed externally. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures predictable performance for enterprise workloads. This server is optimized for large-scale databases, in-memory data stores, and intensive analytical processing.
